title: betula papyrifera subsp. cordifolia
intro: Betula papyrifera subsp. cordifolia, also known as paper birch, is a subspecies of the paper birch tree, characterized by its distinctive white, papery bark and broad, rounded leaves. The medicinal properties of Betula papyrifera subsp. cordifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. Betula papyrifera subsp. cordifolia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
